title: Migration Source Object Dropdown and Sidebar UX Overhaul
story: As a DataTools Pro user mapping migration fields, I want the source object management consolidated into the sidebar panel with clear navigation, so that all mapping inputs are in one place and I don't need a separate popup window.
description:
Major UX overhaul for the migration source object management workflow.

## Bug Fix
- Source object dropdown is empty after creating a new data object until the window is closed and reopened

## UX Redesign
- Remove redundant left/right arrow navigation
- Move the "+" button to the right of the source object dropdown
- Move the gear (edit) icon inside the dropdown area, only visible after an object is selected
- Consolidate the edit popup window into the sidebar panel so all inputs (mapping, logic, notes) are managed in one place
- Add tabs for Mapping, Logic, and Notes in the sidebar
- On Dictionary view, show a Definition tab instead of Logic/Notes
- Use tab navigation (not pills) for Mapping/Logic/Notes sections
- Add multi-line text inputs for Logic and Notes fields

## Behavior
- Edit button opens the sidebar instead of a popup
- Logic and Notes update when navigating between objects via next/previous buttons
- Definition updates on next/previous when in Dictionary view
acceptance_criteria: Source object dropdown populates immediately after creating a new object
Edit button opens the sidebar panel instead of a popup window
Sidebar contains tabs for Mapping, Logic, and Notes
Dictionary view shows Definition tab instead of Logic/Notes
Tab navigation used instead of pills for section switching
Logic and Notes have multi-line text inputs
Next/Previous buttons update Logic, Notes, and Definition per object
Gear icon appears only when an object is selected
Add (+) button positioned to the right of the dropdown
ux_changes: Complete redesign of migration field mapping workflow. Edit popup replaced with consolidated sidebar panel. New tabs for Mapping, Logic, and Notes. Source object dropdown UX cleaned up with repositioned controls. Dictionary view gets a Definition tab.
middleware_changes: Server-side logic for saving/retrieving Logic and Notes fields per source object. Next/Previous navigation updates all fields.
data_model_changes: Logic and Notes fields added to the source object data model.
implementation_notes: Extensive iterative development over several weeks. Initial bug fix for empty dropdown was straightforward. The UX redesign evolved through multiple comment discussions — starting with CSS cleanup, then expanding to a full sidebar consolidation. Tab vs pill navigation choice was refined to use tabs. Dictionary view required separate treatment (Definition tab instead of Logic/Notes).
comment_summary: Long development cycle with 27 comments spanning 2 months. Started as a dropdown bug fix, evolved into a major sidebar UX overhaul. Key decisions: consolidate edit popup into sidebar, use tabs not pills, add Logic/Notes/Definition fields. Developer and Ryan iterated on the layout through multiple rounds of screenshots and feedback.
